ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Apr-26-2022 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Apr-21-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Brent Kato appeared by zoom for Thomas Eilenberger (Petitioner).

The Court asked about bond. Mr. Kato stated that he suggested a $20,000.00 bond, that his understanding is that this is the minimum bond amount for an out of State Personal Representative and that the will waives bond. Mr. Kato also stated that the Decedent's finances were being managed by a licensed Fiduciary (Andrea Leung) and that all of the Decedent's bills are up to date, The Court noted that there is no declaration on file as to that. Mr. Kato stated that Mr. Eilenberger is also the Trustee and one of the Beneficiaries, that there are about half a dozen Beneficiaries and that the Beneficiaries have been identified on attachment 8. The Court stated that if it orders a minimum bond, it is with the understanding that the Personal Representative is out of State, but with Counsel in this State, and that if Counsel changes, the bond will change. The Court granted the Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary. The will dated December 15, 1997 is admitted to probate. Thomas E. Eilenberger is appointed as the Executor of the estate with limited authority. Bond is set at $20,000.00. Karen Ann Simons is the appointed Probate Referee. Status on close of estate is set for April 26, 2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: J. Carranza. Not reported.
